description:
Extend bl close to populate delivered_in opportunistically when it is null on the closing task. In deferred mode, bl review does not write delivered_in (no local squash happened), so by the time bl close runs after the forge merge, the field is still null. Run the existing tag-scan (delivery.rs git_log_find_subject) against the configured target_branch to find the [bl-xxxx]-tagged commit; write it into the close commit on the state branch.

If the tag-scan finds nothing (e.g., the merge SHA hasn't been fetched yet, or the operator forgot the tag in the PR title), allow close to proceed with a warning. The half-push detection in src/commands/half_push.rs already handles "state says closed but main has no matching tag" — we are not regressing that surface, only extending the SHA-resolution path.

WHY: delivered_in is a hint, not source of truth (the README is explicit: the [bl-xxxx] tag in the commit subject is ground truth). The existing fallback in delivery.rs already resolves drifted SHAs via tag-scan. We are extending that same machinery to "hint was never written" — a strict generalization, no new mechanism.

Also accept a manual --delivered <sha> flag on bl close for the case where the operator wants to override the scan (e.g., the forge produced a rebase-merge with multiple commits and they want to point at a specific one).

ACCEPTANCE:
- Closing a local-squash-mode task: behavior unchanged (delivered_in already set by review).
- Closing a deferred-mode task whose [bl-xxxx]-tagged merge is reachable on target_branch: delivered_in populated automatically.
- Closing a deferred-mode task with no matching tag on target_branch: warning emitted; close proceeds; half-push detector behaves identically.
- bl close --delivered <sha> overrides the scan unconditionally.
title: bl close: resolve delivered_in via tag-scan when null
